scenarios.name: Checkout API Error Spike — Missing HTTP Timeouts + N+1 Pattern
scenarios.description: Error rate on /api/checkout jumped from 0.1% to 15%. Datadog shows 502/504 gateway errors, connection pool exhaustion, and p95 latency spike. Suspected application-level bugs in the order-fetching service.

initial_context.incident_43_issue: requests library hung indefinitely because no timeout was specified on external HTTP calls
initial_context.incident_13_issue: Slow performance caused by N+1 pattern — one HTTP request per order instead of batching
initial_context.expected_resolution: Add timeout=5 to all requests.get() calls; batch line-item fetches instead of looping per order
symptoms: Upstream line-items API timing out under load
initial_context.suspected_technical_cause: N+1 HTTP requests per order + missing timeouts in services/orders.py causing connection pool exhaustion

scenarios.investigation_prompt: Investigate the checkout API error spike using Datadog observability tools (logs, metrics, traces). If the root cause is application-level code (missing HTTP timeouts or N+1 query pattern), you MUST: (1) call read_application_code with filepath 'services/orders.py' to inspect the source, (2) rewrite the full corrected file, (3) call create_github_pr to submit the fix, and (4) include the PR URL in your final RCA summary as 'Fix PR: <url>'.
